# src/discovery/filters

File filter implementations for excluding files during codebase discovery using extension detection, gitignore patterns, vendor directory matching, and custom glob rules.

## Contents

### Filter Implementations

**[binary.ts](./binary.ts)** — excludes binary files via `createBinaryFilter(options?: BinaryFilterOptions)` using two-phase detection: extension check against `BINARY_EXTENSIONS` (80+ extensions: .png, .jpg, .zip, .exe, .mp3, .pdf, .woff, .class, .db) then content analysis via `isBinaryFile()` from isbinaryfile package; also excludes files exceeding `maxFileSize` (default 1MB).

**[gitignore.ts](./gitignore.ts)** — parses .gitignore patterns via `createGitignoreFilter(root: string)` using `ignore` library, relativizes absolute paths against root, returns false for paths outside root or empty paths, passes relative paths (no trailing slash) to `ig.ignores()`.

**[vendor.ts](./vendor.ts)** — excludes third-party directories via `createVendorFilter(vendorDirs: string[])` with `DEFAULT_VENDOR_DIRS` covering node_modules, vendor, .git, dist, build, __pycache__, .next, venv, .venv, target; splits patterns into single segments (Set) and path patterns (substring matching).

**[custom.ts](./custom.ts)** — applies user-defined exclusions via `createCustomFilter(patterns: string[], root: string)` using `ignore.Ignore` instance with gitignore-style patterns, relativizes paths and tests against compiled pattern set.

### Filter Orchestration

**[index.ts](./index.ts)** — executes filter chains via `applyFilters(files: string[], filters: FileFilter[], options?)` with 30-worker concurrency (`CONCURRENCY = 30`), short-circuit evaluation (stops at first exclusion), per-filter statistics tracking (`matched`, `rejected`), preserves input order in `FilterResult`, emits `filter:applied` trace events; re-exports all filter constructors and constants.

## Filter Execution Model

Short-circuit evaluation: `applyFilters()` stops checking filters once any `filter.shouldExclude(file)` returns true. Concurrency bounded at 30 workers to prevent file descriptor exhaustion during binary content detection I/O. Excluded files capture `{ path, reason, filter }`. Per-filter stats accumulated across all files.

## Filter Chain Structure

Filters implement `FileFilter` interface from `../types.js` with `{ name: string; shouldExclude(absolutePath: string): boolean }`. Common pattern: normalize root with `path.resolve()`, relativize check paths, return false for paths outside root (starting with '..') or empty paths.

## Detection Strategies

**Extension fast-path**: binary.ts checks `BINARY_EXTENSIONS` before content analysis. **Path segmentation**: vendor.ts splits patterns into single-segment Set lookups vs. substring path pattern matching. **Relativization**: gitignore.ts/custom.ts convert absolute paths to root-relative before pattern matching. **Content fallback**: binary.ts invokes `isBinaryFile(absolutePath)` for unknown extensions, excludes on read errors.

## Dependencies

- `isbinaryfile` package for binary content detection (binary.ts)
- `ignore` library for gitignore-style pattern matching (gitignore.ts, custom.ts)
- `../types.js` for FileFilter, FilterResult, ExcludedFile interfaces
- `../../orchestration/trace.js` for trace event emission (index.ts)
- `../../core/logger.js` for logging (index.ts)
